status: pre-registered; no substrate built, no run executed
question: Does the retrospect agent+skill recover seeded process defects better than bare Claude with identical inputs and tool access?

arms.A: bare: neutral review prompt, no skill/agent content
arms.C: retrospect agent system prompt + /retrospect skill flow, identical inputs

arm_symmetry_control: same model (sonnet, ADR 0006), same substrate cwd, same tool allowance (Read/Grep/Glob/Bash only; all other deny_tools.txt entries stay denied), same transcript/session-log/friction inputs; treatment is content only (ADR 0023)

bar.primary: mean_delta(recall_C - recall_A) > 0, clustered by substrate (6 seeded clusters); 95% CI reported as confidence signal (strong if lower bound > 0, weak otherwise), not a gate
bar.fp_guard: mean_FP_C <= mean_FP_A + 1.0 findings/cell on the 2 clean substrates (prosecutor-confirmed non-real assertions); recall win + guard fail = INCONCLUSIVE-spray, never KEEP
bar.scoring: met_final = min(grader, prosecutor) per matched seed (ADR 0025); recall = found/K per cell

cost.ceiling_basis: set from the ADR 0052 plumbing pilot (../2026-07-12-pdca-plumbing-pilot/README.md): faithful arm-C cell measured $0.375 -> $18.02/48; $30 is ~1.6x that projection, clears the $26.50 conservative both-cells mean, and leaves headroom for the 10-20-commit real substrates the 6-commit pilot under-priced. Supersedes the pre-pilot $15 guess whose gate fired and routed to a fresh /decide per ADR 0052 decision 3.
cost.gate: python3 ../lib/cost_gate.py --cells 48 --pilot-cost-usd <measured...> --ceiling-usd 30 after the ADR 0052 plumbing pilot; nonzero exit halts the grid

blinding.normalizer: lib/blind.py neutral schema {claim, evidence-cite} before grading
blinding.audit: guess-the-arm on 8 sampled normalized cells; accuracy significantly above 0.5 = leak -> ADR 0052 revisit trigger

sequencing: ADR 0052 decision 2: shared plumbing pilot -> this grid -> Instrument 2
